steps.description: Select lowest-numbered unassigned issue in lowest-versioned milestone

parameters.command:
set -euo pipefail

if [ -n "{primary_issue_number}" ] && [ "{primary_issue_number}" != "0" ]; then
  ISSUE_JSON=$(gh issue view {primary_issue_number} --repo {target_repo} --json number,title,labels,assignees,state --jq 'select(.state == "OPEN") | select(([.assignees[].login] | length) == 0) | select(any(.labels[].name; . == "{ok_label}")) | select(all(.labels[].name; . != "{luther_label}")) | {number, title}')
  if [ -n "$ISSUE_JSON" ]; then
    ISSUE_NUMBER=$(echo "$ISSUE_JSON" | jq -r '.number')
    ISSUE_TITLE=$(echo "$ISSUE_JSON" | jq -r '.title')
    gh issue edit "$ISSUE_NUMBER" --repo {target_repo} --add-assignee {assignee} --add-label "{luther_label}" > /dev/null
    jq -n --argjson num "$ISSUE_NUMBER" --arg title "$ISSUE_TITLE" '{number: $num, title: $title}'
    exit 0
  fi
fi


# Query milestones sorted by semver (sort -V)
MILESTONES=$(gh api repos/{target_repo}/milestones --jq '.[].title' | sort -V)

SELECTED_ISSUE=""
FOUND=0
for MILESTONE in $MILESTONES; do
  # List open issues: unassigned, labeled "{ok_label}", not already "{luther_label}"
  ISSUES=$(gh issue list --repo {target_repo} --milestone "$MILESTONE" --state open --assignee "" --label "{ok_label}" --json number,title,labels --limit 50 --jq '[.[] | select(all(.labels[].name; . != "{luther_label}")) | {number, title}] | sort_by(.number)')

  COUNT=$(echo "$ISSUES" | jq 'length')
  if [ "$COUNT" -eq 0 ]; then
    continue
  fi

  for i in $(seq 0 $((COUNT - 1))); do
    NUM=$(echo "$ISSUES" | jq -r ".[$i].number")
    # Skip issues that already have an open PR (REQ-LF-ISSUE-004)
    PR_COUNT=$(gh pr list --repo {target_repo} --search "issue:$NUM" --state open --json number --jq 'length')
    if [ "$PR_COUNT" = "0" ]; then
      SELECTED_ISSUE=$(echo "$ISSUES" | jq -c ".[$i]")
      FOUND=1
      break 2
    fi
  done
done

if [ "$FOUND" = "0" ]; then
  echo "FATAL: No eligible unassigned issues found in any milestone" >&2
  exit 1
fi

ISSUE_NUMBER=$(echo "$SELECTED_ISSUE" | jq -r '.number')
ISSUE_TITLE=$(echo "$SELECTED_ISSUE" | jq -r '.title')

# Claim the issue: assign and label (redirect stdout to avoid polluting JSON output)
gh issue edit "$ISSUE_NUMBER" --repo {target_repo} --add-assignee {assignee} --add-label "{luther_label}" > /dev/null

# Output JSON for context_map extraction (only on success path)
jq -n --argjson num "$ISSUE_NUMBER" --arg title "$ISSUE_TITLE" '{number: $num, title: $title}'
exit 0

steps.description: Ensure repo is checked out, create issue branch, create artifact dir
Fetch issue body and comments, write to artifact files, extract metadata to context

parameters.command:
set -euo pipefail

# Fetch full issue data as JSON
gh issue view {issue_number} --repo {target_repo} --json title,body,comments,url > {artifact_dir}/issue-raw.json

# Write issue body to file
jq -r '.body // ""' {artifact_dir}/issue-raw.json > {artifact_dir}/issue.md

# Write comments with thread structure preserved (REQ-LF-FETCH-003)
jq -r '.comments[] | "## Comment by \(.author.login) at \(.createdAt)\n\n\(.body)\n\n---\n"' {artifact_dir}/issue-raw.json > {artifact_dir}/comments.md

# Emit short metadata as JSON for context_map
jq '{title: .title, url: .url}' {artifact_dir}/issue-raw.json

steps.description: Invoke llxprt to create a plan for the issue

parameters.static_content:
# Implementation Plan

1. This is a docs-only change. Edit only docs/cli/skills.md.
2. Update the interactive /skills note to describe the actual defaults: /skills disable uses workspace scope when the workspace settings path is present/truthy, otherwise user scope; /skills enable removes disabled entries from both workspace and user scopes.
3. Update the terminal llxprt skills examples to match CLI behavior: llxprt skills disable defaults to workspace scope and also supports --scope user/workspace; llxprt skills enable removes disabled entries from both user and workspace scopes.
4. Do not modify source code, lockfiles, generated notice files, or unrelated files.
parameters.prompt: Do not use subagents. Do not ask questions. Read {artifact_dir}/issue.md and {artifact_dir}/comments.md. Do not use shell commands to write the plan. Use the write_file tool to write a concise implementation plan to {artifact_dir}/plan.md, then exit.

steps.description: Invoke llxprt to evaluate the plan

parameters.prompt: Do not use subagents. Do not ask questions. Read the issue in {artifact_dir}/issue.md and the plan in {artifact_dir}/plan.md. Evaluate whether the plan adequately addresses the issue. If the plan is ready, respond with exactly PLAN_APPROVED on its own line. If it needs revision, respond with exactly PLAN_NEEDS_REVISION on its own line, followed by your feedback. Write any detailed feedback to {artifact_dir}/plan-evaluation.md, then exit.

steps.description: Invoke llxprt to implement the plan

parameters.prompt:
Do not use subagents. Do not ask questions. This is a docs-only issue. Read the approved plan in {artifact_dir}/plan.md and implement it.
Edit only docs/cli/skills.md. Do not modify source code, package lockfiles, generated notice files, or unrelated files.
Do not run full project verification commands such as npm run test, npm run build, npm run lint, npm run typecheck, or npm run format:check. The workflow runs those after this step. At most, inspect git diff and run a targeted formatter/check for docs/cli/skills.md if needed.
If {artifact_dir}/implementation-evaluation.md exists, treat it as a required remediation checklist and address every unresolved item before finishing this step.
When the documentation change is complete, respond with exactly IMPLEMENTATION_COMPLETE on its own line, then exit.

steps.description: Subjective review: does the implementation actually solve the issue per plan? (runs AFTER lint/typecheck/test/build pass)

parameters.prompt:
Do not use subagents. Do not ask questions. All deterministic checks (lint, typecheck, test, format, build) have already passed.
Your job is ONLY subjective correctness: read the issue in {artifact_dir}/issue.md and the plan in {artifact_dir}/plan.md, then review the code changes (use git diff against origin/{base_branch}).
Judge only: does this implementation materially solve the issue as described in the plan? Do not require wording to exactly match the plan if the behavior is correctly and consistently documented.
For scope defaults, distinguish interactive /skills behavior from terminal llxprt skills behavior when the implementation does so.
Do NOT re-flag style, formatting, or test issues - those are handled elsewhere.
If the implementation correctly addresses the issue and plan, respond with exactly IMPL_APPROVED on its own line.
If the implementation does not actually solve the issue or materially diverges from the plan, respond with exactly IMPL_NEEDS_WORK on its own line, followed by concise feedback, and also write detailed actionable feedback to {artifact_dir}/implementation-evaluation.md.

steps.description: Run lint, typecheck, test, format, build checks
Invoke llxprt to fix test/lint/build failures

parameters.prompt:
Read the verification failure report in {artifact_dir}/verify-report.json.
Focus only on errors caused by the current branch diff. Start by inspecting `git diff --stat`, `git diff`, and the failing check messages. Do not try to fix pre-existing repository-wide lint warnings that are unrelated to the current diff.
Fix the current branch so the project-level checks can pass. Do not modify package lockfiles, generated notice files, or unrelated files unless they are required by the issue.
If you successfully fix all currently failing checks, respond with exactly REMEDIATION_COMPLETE on its own line as the final output.
If you encounter a system/workflow/tooling problem (shell parse failure, malformed command, missing profile/tool, etc.), respond with exactly REMEDIATION_SYSTEM_ERROR on its own line, followed by a brief reason.
Do not output shell snippets with unmatched quotes.

parameters.command:
set -euo pipefail
git restore --worktree -- packages/vscode-ide-companion/NOTICES.txt 2>/dev/null || true
git add -A -- ':!.luther' ':!.luther/**' ':!packages/vscode-ide-companion/NOTICES.txt'
if git diff --cached --quiet --exit-code; then
  echo "No repository changes to commit" >&2
  exit 1
fi
git commit -m "Fix #{issue_number}: {issue_title}"
git push -u origin issue{issue_number}

steps.description: Invoke llxprt to write a PR description

parameters.prompt: Do not use subagents. Do not ask questions. Read the issue in {artifact_dir}/issue.md and the plan in {artifact_dir}/plan.md. Review the code changes (use git diff origin/{base_branch}). Write a PR description to {artifact_dir}/pr-description.md. The description MUST include 'Fixes #{issue_number}' to auto-close the issue, then exit.

steps.description: Comment on issue, remove label, unassign on failure
